content.problemStatement:
Check if the given year is leap year.

**Note:** A year is a leap year if the following conditions are satisfied:


The year is multiple of 400.
The year is a multiple of 4 and not a multiple of 100.
**Output `YES` if the given year is leap year, else `NO`.**


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single integer YYY - the year.


Output Format

Output on a single line, `YES` if the given year is leap year, else `NO`.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer Y (1900 ≤ Y ≤ 2100) representing a year.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "YES" if the given year is a leap year, otherwise print "NO". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 2004 is divisible by 4 and not by 100, so it is a leap year.
1999 is not divisible by 4, therefore it is not a leap year.
2000 is divisible by 400, making it a leap year.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
YES
NO
YES
NO
YES
NO
YES

solution.approach: A year is a leap year if it satisfies either of the two conditions: (1) it is divisible by 400, or (2) it is divisible by 4 but not by 100. For each test case we simply check these conditions using the modulo operator. The check is O(1) per year, so the total complexity is O(T).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
